Support heap dumps whose per-type index exceeds 2 GB

author: Pierre-Yves Ricau
message:
Remove large heap dump benchmark harness and paging test hooks

Drops the throwaway benchmark scaffolding and the test-only hooks added in
the previous commit, leaving no unused test-only APIs in the codebase. The
paged index is covered by unit tests that build the paged structures
directly (PagedByteArrayTest, PagedSortedBytesMapTest).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.8 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

author: Pierre-Yves Ricau
message:
Add large heap dump benchmark harness and paging test hooks

Temporary scaffolding used to validate the paged index on a real, very
large JVM heap dump:

- GenerateBigHeapDump allocates N objects and dumps the heap via
  HotSpotDiagnosticMXBean.dumpHeap, producing a genuine hprof whose
  instance index exceeds 2 GB.
- BenchmarkOpenHeapDump reports index build time, traversal time, lookup
  throughput and peak heap, with modes to force the single array path
  (reproducing the #2807 NegativeArraySizeException) or force paging.
- generateBigHeapDump / benchmarkOpenHeapDump Gradle tasks.
- forcedEntriesPerPageForTesting / forceSingleArrayForTesting hooks on
  SortedBytesMaps, used by the above and by an end-to-end integration
  test that forces a tiny page size so a small dump exercises the paged
  index through a real HeapGraph.

Removed in the following commit; kept here only for the record.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.8 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

author: Pierre-Yves Ricau
message:
Shard oversized per-record-type indexes across paged byte arrays

A SortedBytesMap was backed by a single ByteArray, which the JVM caps at
~2 GB (Int.MAX_VALUE bytes). For very large heap dumps a per-record-type
index can exceed that limit and the size computation overflows Int,
throwing NegativeArraySizeException while indexing (issue #2807).

SortedBytesMap becomes an interface with two implementations:
ArraySortedBytesMap (the existing single ByteArray) and
PagedSortedBytesMap, which splits storage across power-of-two-entry pages
so the total can exceed the single array limit. Entries never straddle a
page, so each entry stays in one page.

PagedUnsortedByteEntries appends entries across pre-sized pages (no page
ever grows, regardless of id distribution) then sorts the whole logical
array in place with PagedByteArrayTimSort, an in-place port of the AOSP
TimSort whose temp buffer is itself paged. Because the sort is in place,
the pages end up globally id sorted and become the read map's range
partitioned segments, with no separate merge or copy; lookups binary
search a one-key-per-page fence then a single page.

Indexes that fit a single array keep the exact previous code path
(UnsortedByteEntries / ArraySortedBytesMap / ByteArrayTimSort); paging
only engages above the single array limit.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.8 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

author: Simon Marquis
message:
Fix leakcanary-app build on Windows environment

The issue comes from KAPT failing to correctly escape generated proguard files, as you can see in this scan: https://scans.gradle.com/s/v2w3gkl3o7cm6/failures?focused-exception-line=0-2-4-6-8-10-0#1

Bumping Hilt to the next-compatible version.

author: Simon Marquis
message:
Fix LeakCanaryLeakDeobfuscationPluginTest on Windows environments

`local.properties` file creation from `ANDROID_HOME` variables did not properly escaped Windows paths, leading to invalid values.

For instance, if the env variable is `X:\Android`, the resulting file would contain `sdk.dir=X:\Android` which is invalid.

author: Simon Marquis
message:
Fix HeapAnalysisStringRenderingTest on Windows environments

- hprof absolute paths contains backslashes that must be escaped otherwise they are interpreted by the regex compiler
- stacktraces contains platform-dependent new-lines and must be part of the `.*`
- replace greedy quantifiers (`.*`) with lazy ones (`.*?`) for less unnecessary backtracking

Fix primitive array global index in indexedObjectOrNull

author: Pierre-Yves Ricau
message:
Fix primitive array global index in indexedObjectOrNull

indexedObjectOrNull computed the primitive-array branch's global index by
adding primitiveArrayIndex.size instead of objectArrayIndex.size, so the
index was wrong whenever those two sizes differ. objectAtIndex's
require also compared the global index instead of the shifted index,
breaking the round trip. Both are fixed and covered by a new round-trip
test.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.8 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

author: Pierre-Yves Ricau
message:
Rewrite releasing docs for the Central Portal / CI flow

- Drop the obsolete OSSRH account, GPG signing key, and SONATYPE_NEXUS_*
  gradle.properties setup; publishing is automated via GitHub Actions with
  org secrets (note the correct local-publish env vars instead)
- Drive the release from NEW_VERSION/NEXT_VERSION shell variables instead of
  hand-edited NEXT / <NEXT> / REPLACE_WITH_* placeholders (the source of the
  dropped -SNAPSHOT)
- Clarify mkdocs.yml: bump next_release always, promote release only on stable
- Replace the fragile 'close milestone nodes[0]' automation with the real
  per-release milestone practice
- Fix the changelog diff link to use the previous tag
- Mark the Homebrew bump as stable-only (homebrew-core rejects pre-releases)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.8 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

author: Pierre-Yves Ricau
message:
Modernize main CI workflow actions

- Replace archived gradle/gradle-build-action@v2 with the maintained
  gradle/actions/setup-gradle (SHA-pinned to the same v4 release as
  wrapper-validation), preserving build caching
- Bump actions/checkout and actions/setup-java from v3 to v4 (also resolves
  the Node 20 deprecation warnings)
- Add a read-only permissions block to limit the GITHUB_TOKEN scope

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.8 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

author: Pierre-Yves Ricau
message:
Fix snapshot deployment to use Central Portal credentials

The snapshot-deployment job still used the legacy SONATYPE_NEXUS_* secrets,
which the Maven Central Portal snapshot repo rejects with 401. Use the same
SONATYPE_CENTRAL_* token the publish-release workflow uses.

author: py - Pierre Yves Ricau
message:
Merge pull request #2833 from square/release_3.0-alpha-9

Migrate publishing to Maven Central Portal (Kotlin 2.1 / AGP 8.11 / Gradle 8.13)

author: Pierre-Yves Ricau
message:
Migrate publishing to Maven Central Portal

OSSRH (s01.oss.sonatype.org) was decommissioned, so publishing via
vanniktech's SonatypeHost.S01 no longer works. Publish through the
CENTRAL_PORTAL host from a GitHub Actions workflow, like square/papa.

Central Portal support requires vanniktech 0.32.0, which requires Kotlin
>= 1.9.20. Bump Kotlin 1.8.21 -> 1.9.25 (and the Compose compiler
1.4.7 -> 1.5.15 to match). AGP (8.2.2) and Gradle (8.10.2) are left
unchanged on purpose: Kotlin 2.x would force an AGP bump whose Unified
Test Platform fails to talk to the emulator on our CI ("Failed to receive
the UTP test results"), breaking all instrumentation tests, and it is not
needed for publishing.

- build.gradle.kts: SonatypeHost.S01 -> CENTRAL_PORTAL (automaticRelease)
- Modernize stdlib calls surfaced by the Kotlin bump: Char.toUpperCase ->
  uppercaseChar, String.toLowerCase(Locale) -> lowercase(Locale), and a
  non-null ViewModelProvider.Factory.create override
- apiDump: enums gain getEntries() (stable Enum.entries since Kotlin 1.9;
  additive, non-breaking)
- Add .github/workflows/publish-release.yml (workflow_dispatch), hardened
  with a read-only permissions block and no unpinned/archived actions
- Update docs/releasing.md for the GitHub Actions / Central Portal flow

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.8 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
